title: Minimum Cost to Make at Least One Valid Path in a Grid
hints: Think of the grid as a graph where each cell connects to its neighbors. Existing arrows are free edges; changing arrows creates edges with cost 1.
This is a shortest path problem on a graph with edge weights 0 and 1. Use 0-1 BFS or Dijkstra's algorithm to find the minimum cost.
Initialize distances to infinity and start at (0,0) with cost 0. Process neighbors: free moves push to front, paid moves push to back of the deque.
